claude.reason: Use a relative path or a workspace path (e.g. `grep pat src/file.py`, `rg pat ~/<workspace>/repo/`). Relative, workspace (`~/<workspace>/...`, `/Users/<me>/<workspace>/...`), and `/tmp` paths are allowed. A path outside those safe zones -- arbitrary absolute (`/etc/...`), bare `~`, or `..` traversal -- is rejected for scope control: it can flood context with an unbounded scan. To read one known file by an out-of-zone absolute path use the Read tool. Pipeline filters on bounded stdout stay allowed (`... | grep pat`).

codex.reason: Use a relative path or a workspace path (e.g. `grep pat src/file.py`, `rg pat ~/<workspace>/repo/`). Relative, workspace (`~/<workspace>/...`, `/Users/<me>/<workspace>/...`), and `/tmp` paths are allowed. A path outside those safe zones -- arbitrary absolute (`/etc/...`), bare `~`, or `..` traversal -- is rejected for scope control. For one authorized file in another readable working directory, run `rg -n '^' <relative-file>` from that directory. Pipeline filters on bounded stdout stay allowed (`... | grep pat`).

claude.reason: Use `git ls-files <pathspec>` to list candidate files, then use the Read tool on targeted files. `grep` and `rg` remain useful as pipeline filters on already-bounded command output (`git ls-files | grep pat`). For broad or structured content searches write a `_temp.py` helper that starts from a bounded candidate list (e.g. `git ls-files`) and run it with `source source_me.sh && python3 _temp.py`. `git grep` is denied to keep repo content search on the bounded workflow: list candidates first, then inspect targeted files. Scope-control rule, not anti-grep.

codex.reason: Use `git ls-files <pathspec>` to list candidates, then `rg -n <pattern> <relative-file>` to inspect targeted files. Use `rg -n '^' <relative-file>` when every line is needed. For broad or structured searches write a `_temp.py` helper that starts from a bounded candidate list and run it with `source source_me.sh && python3 _temp.py`. `git grep` is denied to keep repo content search bounded. Scope-control rule, not anti-grep.

claude.reason: Use `git ls-files <pathspec>` to identify candidate files, then use the Read tool on the relevant files. `pcregrep`, `ack`, and `ag` with a file path argument are rejected for the same scope-control reason as `grep`/`rg` with a file path; piped forms on bounded stdout stay allowed.

codex.reason: Use `git ls-files <pathspec>` to identify candidates, then `rg -n <pattern> <relative-file>` on the relevant files. `pcregrep`, `ack`, and `ag` with a file path argument are rejected for the same scope-control reason as `grep`/`rg` with a file path; piped forms on bounded stdout stay allowed.

claude.reason: Ask the user to run any scripted app step. For app screenshots, use a dedicated screenshot helper that targets the window for you.

codex.reason: Ask the user to run any scripted app step. For app screenshots, use ~/nsh/easy-screenshot/run.sh --application <App>, which targets the window for you.

claude.reason: Invoke the actual tool, not its name typed into Bash. `Read` reads a file; `Edit`/`Write` modify files; `Task`/`WebFetch`/`WebSearch` are first-class tool calls. For file search use `git ls-files <pathspec>` plus the Read tool. Typing `Grep`, `Glob`, `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Task`, `WebFetch`, or `WebSearch` into Bash runs whatever (if anything) is on PATH by that name, not the Claude Code tool.

codex.reason: Tool labels are not shell commands. Use the tools exposed in the current session. For bounded text inspection, use `git ls-files <pathspec>` to find candidates and `rg -n <pattern> <relative-file>` or `rg -n '^' <relative-file>` to read them. Typing `Grep`, `Glob`, `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Task`, `WebFetch`, or `WebSearch` into Bash runs whatever (if anything) is on PATH by that name.

claude.reason: Look up the window id in a separate step, then pass that literal id to screencapture. A screenshot helper script can target the window for you.

codex.reason: Look up the window id in a separate step, then pass that literal id to screencapture. Or use ~/nsh/easy-screenshot/run.sh --application <App>, which targets the window for you.

claude.reason: Use project paths under ~/projects/ or scratch files in /tmp/. Editing system files (`/etc/`, `/usr/`, `/opt/`, `/System/`, `/Library/`) is rejected.

codex.reason: Use project paths under ~/nsh/ or scratch files in /tmp/. Editing system files (`/etc/`, `/usr/`, `/opt/`, `/System/`, `/Library/`) is rejected.

claude.reason: Use project paths under ~/projects/ or scratch files in /tmp/. Writing to system directories (`/etc/`, `/usr/`, `/opt/`, `/System/`, `/Library/`) is rejected.

codex.reason: Use project paths under ~/nsh/ or scratch files in /tmp/. Writing to system directories (`/etc/`, `/usr/`, `/opt/`, `/System/`, `/Library/`) is rejected.

claude_only.reason: For line-matching in files, use `git ls-files <pathspec>` to identify candidates and use the Read tool on targeted files. Piped `grep` or `rg` on already-bounded stdout stays allowed (`<allowed cmd> | grep pat`, `<allowed cmd> | rg pat`). For field extraction from piped output use `cut`. For structured parsing write a `_temp.py` helper that starts from a bounded candidate list such as `git ls-files`, then run it with `source source_me.sh && python3 _temp.py`. `awk`/`gawk`/`mawk` is denied including pipeline leaves -- no pipe exception.
Inspect the script with the Read tool. To execute, use `bash script.sh` or ask for user approval. `bash`/`sh`/`zsh -n` syntax-check is rejected; the shell is not the right tool for script analysis.

Invoke the Read tool: file_path=<path>, with optional offset and limit. For piped stdout, `... | head -5`, `... | tail -5`, `... | cat` are still allowed. `cat`/`head`/`tail` against a file path is rejected because Read provides line numbers and bounded slicing.

Invoke the Read tool: file_path=<path>, with optional offset and limit. `less <file>` is rejected because Read provides line numbers and bounded slicing; the pipeline form `... | less` is not the same shape and is not auto-allowed either.

Invoke the Read tool: file_path=<path>, offset=<start>, limit=<count> to extract a line range from a file. For piped stdout, `... | sed -n '10,20p'` is still allowed. `sed -n` against a file path is rejected because Read does file-range extraction directly.

codex_only.reason: Sensitive environment and secret files are outside the editable policy surface.
Keep patches inside the workspace or /tmp. System paths are outside the editable policy surface.
